title: P3.2 Skills Recall example corpus v1
purpose: Small representative corpus of bounded recall packets for real work after the P3.1 execution-truth layer.

scenarios.why_it_matters: Shows the resume-style packet shape when execution truth is already restored and the next question is which local protocol to read before continuing.

input_context.task_title: Resume the startup recovery follow-up after restart
input_context.user_request: Confirm the next bounded follow-up before touching shared state again.
input_context.representative_mode: resume-style example grounded in the existing startup recovery artifacts

active_item.title: Resume the startup recovery follow-up after restart
active_item.notes: P3.1 already picked the trustworthy work item; this packet only restores the smallest supporting context for the next bounded step.

recall_candidates.why_recalled: The recovered item already points at this note, so it is the most trustworthy first anchor for the bounded follow-up.
The follow-up still sits inside shared Qxiaohu and Erliang collaboration, so the structured handoff and stop-and-escalate defaults are the smallest reusable operating contract to reload.
This note is the clearest local boundary that says startup recovery still owns execution truth while Skills Recall only restores supporting context.

boundary.startup_recovery_status: already decided by P3.1 doctor or resume-hottest before this packet is produced

scenarios.why_it_matters: Captures the review or rework case where the main risk is not missing execution truth but missing reusable guardrails for ETA sync, readability, and handoff discipline.

input_context.task_title: Tighten the next reviewer rework pass
input_context.user_request: Enter rework, sync an ETA, and make sure the artifact is readable before review.
input_context.representative_mode: task-context example grounded in the current review gate artifact

active_item.title: Tighten the next reviewer rework pass
active_item.notes: The packet exists to surface rework discipline before any new review-round status message or handoff.

recall_candidates.why_recalled: The current task already names this artifact, so it remains the first bounded anchor for the rework pass.
The user request explicitly carries rework, ETA, and readability risk, so the retrospective is the most direct reusable runbook to surface before acting.
The structured collaboration rules carry the compact reminders that chat status is not execution status and that rework should sync an ETA before handoff.

boundary.startup_recovery_status: task context supplied directly; no startup state change performed

scenarios.why_it_matters: Shows the bounded packet when the task text clearly points to one installed skill and one local handoff runbook, without reading every available skill.

input_context.task_title: Patch the cloud doc handoff note
input_context.user_request: Open the cloud doc link, patch the latest handoff summary, and leave a clean doc update.
input_context.representative_mode: task-context example grounded in the existing kickoff handoff note

active_item.title: Patch the cloud doc handoff note
active_item.notes: The active item is stable already; the open question is which single installed skill and which local checklist should be reloaded before editing the cloud doc.

recall_candidates.why_recalled: The task path already names the exact kickoff handoff artifact, so that note stays the first anchor.
The task ends in a handoff-ready update, so the standard execution checklist is the right local runbook to surface before editing and relaying the result.
The task text mentions a cloud doc link, which is the clearest single-domain hint to reload the installed feishu-doc skill before action.

boundary.startup_recovery_status: task context supplied directly; no startup state change performed
